prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "弹力带绕踝侧向行走" (banded lateral walk / monster walk) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person performing a banded lateral walk: standing in a quarter-squat position with knees slightly bent (about 30 degrees of knee flexion) and hips pushed back. A loop resistance band is wrapped around both ankles, kept taut. The person is mid-step, taking a wide lateral step to the right with the right foot, while the left leg remains slightly bent and stable. The band creates outward tension that the gluteus medius works against. Torso upright. Hands at hips. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus medius (especially of the moving and stabilizing legs). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and quadriceps and ankle stabilizers. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "股四头肌 / 踝稳定肌" pointing to the quadriceps and ankle stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
